observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in the verification/validation of the dormant and/or unnecessary accounts for possible closing the same and/or reclassification to other existing bank accounts under the same fund.

We recommended that Management re-examine and consider the update of its Local Revenue Code to generate additional income by revising the existing ordinances. Require the Local Finance Committee to consider what provisions of the Revenue Code that needs to be retained, deleted and added to have a positive and sound projections of its financial requirements. Further, updating and rationalizing the tax rates of the Revenue Code once every five (5) years as embodied under Section 191 of the Local Government Code ensures the sustainability and resilience of its various local taxes.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management adopt the following measures to ensure the effective GAD implementation in the LGU:

a.	Institutionalize and/or update a working and functional GFPS or a similar GAD mechanism to catalyze and accelerate gender mainstreaming within the Municipality;

b.	Ensure that members of the GFPS set regular meetings or conferences to objectively formulate its periodic plans and objectives and to assess, monitor, and evaluate implementation of GAD PPAs; and

c.	Prioritize measures on the formulation and passage of GAD Code as prescribed by the GAD laws and regulations.

We recommended the mainteinance of stock cards and supplies ledger cards for the proper monitoring of balances and the necessity of requisition of particular inventory items.

Furthermore, it was recommended that specific offices with ending inventories, more specifically with the RHU, having medicines and medical supplies, and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office having welfare goods for distribution, to conduct its own physical inventory-taking of inventories at the end of each month and submit the reports to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of the SSMI to be submitted to the Accounting Unit for recording of the appropriate expenses.

Lastly, we recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to ensure that the conduct of inventory-taking for inventory items shall be made, and the same shall be properly and adequately planned through the preparation of the Inventory Plan to achieve an accurate, complete, and efficient result.

We reitereated our previous audit recommendations that Management direct the:

Accountable Officers with existing receivables from the LGU to compensate their unsettled accounts with such claims, and the balances thereof shall be requested for write-off to the Commission on Audit following the procedures embodie in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Local Chief Executive to prepare request for write-off for the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant to the same given Circular.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester, or upon the dates prescribed by the latter.

Require the Municipal Engineer to submit weather bulletin from the PAGASA to the Resident Auditors instead of the manually prepared weather chart to justify the Suspension Orders issued for the frequency of rains; and


For the absence of valid proof in the issuance of suspension orders, require the Municipal Accountant to compute and impose liquidated damages at least equal to one-tenth (1/10) of one percent (1%) of the cost of the unperformed portion of every day of delay.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:
Inventory Committee to:
Conduct the annual physical inventory of PPEs within the jurisdiction of the LGU and relieve each member of their regular functions to devote themselves in completing the same;
Prepare the PIP, and submit the same to the Municipal Mayor for approval to ensure that the conduct of inventory-taking is properly and adequately planned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Use pre-numbered property stickers bearing the vital information on the PPE items, such as
Description of the Property
Model Number
Serial Number
Acquisition Date / Cost
Person Accountable
Space for the Validation / Signature of the Inventory Committee
in the conduct of physical inventory and attach the same to the prominent and secured portion of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification;
Prepare a consolidated RPCPPE and submit to the MGSO and Municipal Accountant for reconciliation of PPE items with their corresponding records and investigate discrepancies thereof; and
Strictly conduct the inventory taking in accordance with the procedures prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Municipal Accountant to record adjustments after completion of proper reconciliation to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, supported with appropriate documents.

The Municipal Accountant:
To adjust the entries made by reversing the previously recorded accounts. The following entries given hereunder:

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts in the verification/validation of the dormant and/or unnecessary account to close the same and/or transfer the balances to the appropriate bank accounts.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the BAC to:
Properly examine the Bid Documents submitted by the bidders during preliminary examination of bids to ensure the completeness and validity of the eligibility requirements prior to recommending the award of contracts or purchase orders;
Moving forward, issue the NOA only upon full compliance with performance security requirements;
Ensure that the winning bidders post-performance security within the prescribed period prior to endorsement of procurement documents to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of PO; and
Strictly require the TWG to conduct and exercise utmost prudence in the post-qualification of the LCB to properly determine whether the LCB complies with all the eligibility requirements and conditions specified in the RIRR and provide evaluation data on the post-qualification of the bidders such as a) ascertain that the bidder should not have any tax liability with the Government from the time of submission of its bid up to the time of contact award and, b) the bidder’s Audited FS be submitted subject for validation during the conduct of the said post-qualification.
Also, we recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer as the acting GSO to ensure that prior to preparation and issuance of contracts or POs for the procurement of goods, the performance security is posted by the winning bidders.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct:
The BAC to:
Stop preparing a consolidated PR;
Adopt a strategy to ensure that similar items/nature of goods to be procured indicated in the PPMPs of end-users are properly consolidated into the APP;
Strictly conduct the procurement of goods in accordance with the RIRR of RA 9184.
The GSO, BAC and other department heads to come up with an efficient strategy in preparing and processing of PRs for the procurement of goods in accordance with NGAS and RIRR of RA 9184 while ensuring that goods of similar nature or items and needed at about the same time are procured thru public bidding save for any exceptions.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
To require the business owners to submit a sworn statement of gross sales/receipts/Audited FS if applicable, and assessment should be based thereon; and
Conduct examination on books of accounts if in the validation of the personnel of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office that there will be under declaration of gross receipts by taxpayers to establish effective enforcement and collection of taxes, fees and charges as provided in the Ordinance.

Recommendations
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations, and the management agreed to prioritize the registration of the real properties under the Torrens Title System:
Relative to lands without proof of ownership but identified as owned by the LGU and lands covered only with Deed of Sale as stated in the presented table.
Designate a certain person to be in charge of obtaining the necessary documents for the title of these lands;
Provide adequate appropriation to cover the expenses relevant thereto;
Direct the Municipal Assessor and Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate/assist the designated person to expedite the registration of these LGU land parcels and to submit the required documents to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.
Relative to lands with Original Certificate of Title and Deed of Sale as presented in the table, but not recorded in the books of accounts
The Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Accountant with Tax Declaration of the lands; and
Upon receipt of the Tax Declaration of the lands, the Municipal Accountant to record the value of the land based on the current market value reflected thereto.

Recommendations
We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Engineer to immediately conduct thorough review and evaluation of unimplemented projects and expedite the preparation of necessary documents to facilitate the request for procurement and implementation of these projects.
Local Finance Committee to re-assess the projects funded under the 20 per cent continuing appropriations as to viability, feasibility, and relevance.
Municipal Budget Officer and MPDC to inform the Municipal Engineer of the changes continuously and regularly in the projects for immediate implementation of the projects.
Lastly, we recommended, and Management agreed to ensure identification of projects to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF are well planned, and procurement-and-implementation ready to avoid implementation issues such as ROW, soil and site problems, and other issues that need to be addressed during the pre-engineering activities.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) endorse the proposal of GAD Code for the LGU supported by in-depth study and/or analysis of the gender issues of the women, men and children of the Municipality of Sibutad and submit the same to the Sanggunian Bayan for enactment into Ordinance thru the Chairperson, Committee on Women, Children and Family Welfare.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Capacitate the LGU GFPS by undergoing GAD related trainings, including gender analysis, specifically on the administration of HGDG to properly implement the LGU’s GPB in addressing the actual and immediate gender issues/concerns of the Municipality.
Direct the GFPS in attributing PPAs for GAD, to conduct gender analysis thru through the use of HGDG test to determine the gender responsiveness of the LGU’s regular/flagship PPAs and attached the results of HGDG to the GPB.
Direct the GFPS to conduct HGDG test thru the PIMME checklist for GAD attributed PPAs to determine the actual cost of implementation attributed to GAD and support the same in the preparation of GAD AR.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the GFPS to follow up/make representation to the Office of the Municipal Mayor on the status of the submitted resolution relative to the above matter and the same endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children and Elderly of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan, the passing of an ordinance localizing RA 11313, which if passed, shall be posted in conspicuous places. Further, include in the ordinance the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions, b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof, and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:
Municipal Accountant to provide to the MDRRM Officer status of unexpended balances of QRF and DRRM-MOOE transferred to the Trust Fund every year;
the MDRRM Officer:
Present the funds to the Municipal Mayor for proposal of PPAs, submit the LDRRMF Budgetary Program to the MDRRM Council for adoption, and update the LDRRMF Investment Plan of the current year, upon receipt of the Status of Unexpended balances from the Municipal Accountant.
Review, and evaluate the PPAs listed in the LDDRMFIP as to its feasibility and implementability. Should otherwise, recommend revisions or amendments to LDRRMF Investment Plan to reflect new or realigned PPAs. The PPAs should focus on the disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness and recovery and rehabilitation to achieve the purpose of the disaster fund; and
Monitor the implementation of PPAs in the LDRRMF Investment Plan to ensure that PPAs are implemented within the prescribed timelines. This includes monitoring the accomplishment of PPAs charged under TF.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Cause the immediate reversion of the CY 2018 unutilized balance of the LDDRMF amounting to P113,215.22 to the unappropriated surplus of the GF for reappropriation to other social services; and
Transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF in the GF to TF as at year-end and not merely as set-up of obligation, moving forward.

We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to carry the following courses of action to have a correct and reliable balances of the RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements:

a.The Acting Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year the Master List / Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) showing the names of the taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible, which will serve as basis of the latter for the recording of the RPT and SET Receivables.

b.The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per Master List of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Unit to:

a.Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts, as provided above; and

b.Strictly observe the accrual basis of accounting in recording its financial statements, where applicable.

We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to improve its communication strategies in ensuring that delinquent taxpayers receive the necessary notices regarding their tax obligations. These communication materials should include clear and concise messages highlighting the importance of paying taxes, the repercussions of delinquencies, and the benefits of compliance.

We further recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer post a complete notice of delinquency at the main entrance of the municipal hall and its publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each of its barangays. This step will help ensure that taxpayers are aware of their delinquencies and the consequences of non-payment.

Lastly, we recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer conduct tax campaigns specifically encouraging taxpayers to pay their tax obligations in a timely manner.

Non-completion by the Inventory Committee of the annual physical count and reconciliation of the results thereto with the property and accounting records of the property custodian and accounting unit, hence, verification and adjustments for discrepancies cannot be made.

Direct the Inventory Committee to prioritize the completion of the physical inventory-taking of all the LGU’s assets and prepare the RPCPPE for each type of property, plant and equipment in accordance with the prescribed format, and as provided in the detailed guidelines and procedures of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

Upon the completion of the physical inventory-taking and the preparation of the RPCPPE, a reconciliation is to be made between the properties appearing in the inventory report, property cards, and in the books of the accounting unit. The reconciliation shall be completed within ten (10) days from the rendition of the said RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.

Lastly, missing PPEs established during the physical count shall be investigated and demand letters are to be issued to those accountable officers concerned, including the requirement of written explanation for the said losses.

b. Initial recognition of the Road Networks account in the Property, Plant and Equipment was undertaken without adhering to the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008, thus, the said account with a carrying amount of ₱52.536 million is unreliable, and no proper disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements of the account was made thereof.

Management, through the following heads of offices take the following duties and responsibilities for the initial recognition of the Road Networks in the books of accounts of the municipality, as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008:

Reporting Guidelines:

The General Services Officer at the end of the accounting period to render a Report on Local Road Network of the local government unit concerned. (Annex A)

The local road network system is to be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Agency. (Annex B)

The Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the local government unit. (Annex C)

Duties and Responsibilities:

Local Accountant
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the road network and its components in the general ledger, and the Local Road Network Ledger Car (Annex D), respectively;

Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for roads, working paper on the distribution costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement costs for road components with no available cost per registry;

Keep and maintain subsidiary records for road and road components for every road network; and

Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and

Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all the roads and its components. (Annex E)


Municipal Engineer
Provide the Local Accountant and the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

Local Chief Executive
Enjoin the department heads’ compliance with the requirements of the Circular.

c. Accuracy and reliability of the Land account totaling ₱1.905 million as of December 30, 2020 could not be ascertained due to the absence of Transfer Certificates of Title (TCTs) or its equivalent in the name of the Municipal Government, not in conformity with Section 23(2), Title I-B, Book V of the 1987 Administrative Code and Section 449 of the GAAM, Volume I.

Management to prioritize the registration of their real properties under the Torrens Title System by:

Providing an adequate appropriation to cover the expenses relevant thereto;

Engaging in negotiated procurement, when deemed necessary, for the survey of the parcels of land owned by the municipality, as the first step for the registration of the same; and

Directing the Municipal Assessor and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with each other and fast track the other necessary processes/ documentation for the registration of the different parcels of land owned by the LGU, including securing the Transfer Certificates of Title thereof.

Moreover, the Municipal Assessor to assess the fair market values of the different Land accounts and parcels of land owned by the agency and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the recording of the same in the books of the LGU.

Lastly, turn over to the Acting Municipal Treasurer, as the General Services Officer of the municipality, custody of all the secured ownership documents, especially the Transfer Certificates of Title, of all parcels of land owned by the LGU for safe-keeping.

Direct the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, Municipal Assessor and Acting Municipal Treasurer to closely coordinate with each other to facilitate the transfer of the road lots in the name of the Municipal Government and strictly secure ownership documents as a pre-requisite in the implementation of road development projects.

Moreover, the municipality to appropriate funds needed to secure the TCTs of the above-subjected lots, including any other lots used as municipal public roads.

Consequently, the secured ownership documents shall be given to the custody of the Acting Municipal Treasurer, as the General Services Officer of the municipality.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection efforts on delinquent Real Property Taxes and institute appropriate administrative and judicial remedies, especially to those taxes who are past due for more than five (5) years, to enforce tax compliance from delinquent taxpayers.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to prepare and submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format for approval and submission by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester.

To comply AO No. 70, s. 2003 dated April 14, 2003 on the creation of Internal Audit Service and implementing guidelines under DBM Circular No. 2004-4 dated March 22, 2004 on the organization and staffing of Internal Auditing Units.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to use the perpetual inventory method in recording supplies and materials and other inventory items, and follow the correct recording of such account based on the appropriate reports and source documents, i.e. AIR, SSMI, etc., in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
Moreover, we recommended the maintenance of stock cards and supplies ledger cards for the proper monitoring of balances and the necessity of requisition of particular inventory items.
Furthermore, it was recommended that specific offices with ending inventories, more specifically with the Rural Health Unit (RHU), having medicines and medical supplies, and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO), having welfare goods for distribution, to conduct its own physical inventory-taking of inventories at the end of each month and submit the reports to the Municipal Treasurer for the preparation of the SSMI to be submitted to the Accounting Unit for recording of the appropriate expenses.
Lastly, we recommended that Management direct the Inventory Committee to ensure that the conduct of inventory-taking for inventory items shall be made, and the same shall be properly and adequately planned through the preparation of the Inventory Plan to achieve an accurate, complete and efficient result.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the following:
Municipal Accountant
To adjust the entries made by recording as an expense those capitalized items following the entries given hereunder:
(Debit) Appropriate Expense Account			xxx
(Credit) Office Supplies Inventory			xxx
To adjust the balance of the retained earnings account only when the whereabouts of these semi-expendable items are established and complete documentation have been provided; and
To help the General Services Office ensure that all those expended semi-expendable properties were issued with the required ICS to establish accountability.
Municipal Treasurer
Complete the physical inventory-taking for both inventories and PPE to establish existence of the said items;
Issue the appropriate ICS to the accountable officers of the semi-expendable properties to establish accountability; and
Safe-keep a copy of the ICS issued to the accountable officers for future references.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management create or update the composition of the Inventory Committee, having all department heads as members, for the purpose of conducting the physical inventory-taking and the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the LGU.
Moreover, we recommended that Management direct the:
Inventory Committee to –
Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP), and submit the same to the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the conduct of inventory-taking is properly and adequately planned pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Submit a copy of the approved PIP to COA at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
Use pre-numbered property stickers bearing the vital information of the PPE items, such as
Description of the Property
Model Number
Serial Number
Acquisition Date/Cost
Person Accountable
Space for the Validation/Signature of the Inventory Committee
in the conduct of physical inventory and attach the same to the prominent and secured portion of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification.
Allow all department heads, as members of the Committee, to take charge in the inventory-taking for each departments, and submit a copy of the RPCPPE under its respective jurisdictions;
The Chairperson of the Committee shall prepare a consolidated RPCPPE which will then be the basis for the reconciliation of the PPE items appearing in the accounting records; and
Should there be any discrepancies in the accounting records and the consolidated RPCPPE, investigate, identify and address the nature and causes of such variances and hold responsible/accountable the designated officers/custodians in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
Municipal Treasurer as the Designated Municipal General Services Officer to –
Use pre-numbered property tags or control stickers to all PPE containing the information required under Section 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and attach the same to the prominent and secured portion of the inventoried PPE for proper and easy identification.
Ensure that all issuances of PPE items are covered with Property Acknowledgment Receipts (PARs) as prescribed under Section 5.6 of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Renew/Update issued PARs every three (3) years after issuance or whenever there is a change in custodianship to establish proper accountability.
Municipal Accountant to record adjustments after completion of proper reconciliation to establish the correct balances of PPE and other affected accounts, with the presence of valid supporting documents.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Establish an Inventory Committee dedicated only to conduct physical count of Local Road Network;
Before conducting the physical count, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer reconcile their records to ensure that the road network system recorded in the books is reflected in the Inventory of Road Network that shall be maintained by the Municipal Engineer’s Office;
There, direct the Inventory Committee to start with the road network system recorded in the books. In the conduct of the physical count, determine each component of the local roads, its costs or fair market value (if without identified costs); and
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant to –
Prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) separately from each of the road components, namely: a) road lot; b) road pavement; c) drainage and slope protection structures; and d) other miscellaneous structures.
Request records of the municipal road lots from the Municipal Engineering’s Office and Municipal Assessor’s Office and record the same at its fair market value; and
Complete the disclosure of the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements by providing the total cost of the road networks of the Municipality.
Furthermore, it was recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer/MGSO to prepare, maintain and regularly update the Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPC), in accordance with the prescribed format per COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Lastly, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records to determine the actual municipal roads, assess the same, and forward the information to the Municipal Accountant for the recording in the books.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that management prioritize the registration of their real properties under the Torrens Title System by:
Creating a task force for the identification of the parcels of land owned by the LGU and the conduct of the necessary steps in the registration of such;
Providing an adequate appropriation to cover the expenses relevant thereto;
Engaging in negotiated procurement, when deemed necessary and warranted, for the survey of the parcels of land, as the first step for the registration; and
Directing the Municipal Assessor and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with each other and fast track the other necessary processes/documentation for the registration of the different parcels of land owned by the LGU, including securing the Transfer Certificates of Title thereof.
Moreover, it was recommended that the Municipal Assessor assess the fair market values of the different unrecognized Land accounts and parcels of land owned by the agency and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording of the same in the books of the LGU, to provide a more reliable balances in the financial statements.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our previous audit recommendations that Management direct the:
Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review, with the involvement of other concerned personnel, and analyze the details of the receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and send demand letters to persons who have known address to facilitate collections;
Accountable Officers with existing receivables from the LGU to compensate their unsettled accounts with such claims, and only the balances thereof shall be requested for write-off to the Commission on Audit following the procedures embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
Local Chief Executive to prepare request for write-off to the Commission on Audit for the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant to the same given Circular.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, through its MDRRMO-Designate to:
Update its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan by focusing on the projects, programs and activities requiring the highest priority in terms of urgency and impact on the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management; and
Monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF to effectively implement the planned PPAs within the targeted schedule and to ensure the optimal utilization of the fund.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management re-examine and consider the update of its Local Revenue Code to generate additional income by revising the existing ordinances. Require the Local Finance Committee to consider on what provisions of the Revenue Code that needs to be retained, deleted and added to have a positive and sound projections of its financial requirements. Further, updating and rationalizing the tax rates of the Revenue Code once every five (5) years as embodied under Section 191 of the Local Government Code ensure the sustainability and resilience of its various local taxes.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to conduct tax campaigns in order to inform the constituents about the necessity and sanctions of paying their real property taxes.
Moreover, it was recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer send demand letters to the delinquent taxpayers for the payment of their unpaid taxes.
Lastly, after sending three demand letters and the taxpayers does not make the appropriate action, consider requesting from the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to issue the necessary tax bill for the same.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management adopt the following measures to ensure effective GAD implementation in the LGU:
Institutionalize and/or update a working and functional GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) or a similar mechanism to catalyze and accelerate  gender mainstreaming within the municipality;
Ensure that members of the GFPS set regular meetings or conferences to objectively formulate its periodic plans and objectives and to assess, monitor and evaluate implementation of GAD PPAs; and
Prioritize measures on the formulation and passage of GAD Code as prescribed by the GAD laws and regulations.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to complete the preparation of the Procurement Monitoring Reports in its prescribed format, and have the same approved and submitted by the Municipal Mayor to the Government Procurement Policy Board within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester, or upon the dates prescribed by the latter.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Conduct thorough review, with the involvement of other concerned personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and send demand letters to persons who have known address to facilitate collections;

For Accountable Officers with existing receivables from the LGU to compensate their unsettled accounts with such claims, and the balances thereof shall be requested for write-off to the Commission on Audit following the procedures embodies in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

Prepare request for write-off for the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected which should be duly supported with certified relevant documents pertaining to the accounts pursuant to the same given Circular.

Management to direct the following personnel:

Municipal Treasurer
Ensure the necessary information/data are to be provided in the AIR to substantiate the deliveries of the goods, supplies and/or materials purchased by the Municipality, and that the DRs issued by the suppliers are the actual amount of items delivered for such date of delivery;

Ensure that the RIS be reflective of the actual issuance of the goods, supplies and materials to the end-users, and not just a uniform amount as presented in the PO; and

Ensure that the POs shall contain the Dates of Delivery and the Payment Terms which will be the basis for charging the suppliers for late deliveries of purchases.

Municipal Accountant
Make the necessary adjusting entries of the on-hand inventories that were expensed outright, after validating the same through actual physical inventory-taking.

Head Inspector / Inspection Committee
Ensure that the DRs are reflective of the actual amounts/items delivered and bears the date of delivery on its face; and

Ensure that the items delivered are compliant with the quantity, quality and specifications provided in the PO and never accept deliveries that are of inferior quality and/or defective to be able to obtain optimal benefits of the same.

LDRRMO / MHO / MSWDO
Coordinate with the Property Custodian/GSO/Acting Municipal Treasurer and provide the Audit Team with the distribution lists with signatures of beneficiaries, logbooks, and any other records or reports to substantiate the unaccounted items of relief goods, medicines, medical supplies and/or medical equipment provided in Appendix C, and provide reconciliation of the same; and

Investigate any discrepancy thereof and provide the Audit Team with written justification to substantiate such discrepancy.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer to execute a yearly Contract of Lease embodied in a public instrument for every market stall rental and follow the standards/guidelines provided under COA Circular No. 88-282A to ensure the interest of both parties are protected, ambiguities and/or disagreements are avoided, and peaceful operation of the municipal public market is sustained.

Schedule of payment of rental fees of stalls be judiciously updated to be able to monitor and impose surcharges for late payments.

Secure the authority from the Sanggunian and approval from the Local Chief Executive of the special savings deposit account and consider forming part of the said fund for the next budget preparation for the implementation of programs, projects and activities beneficial to the public as a whole so that the needed services of the constituents may not be sacrificed in favor of earning interest income.

Consequently, if it can be proven that the said fund is a cash back up for an existing unimplemented project, management to secure the necessary documentation and revert the said fund to the general current account maintained by the municipality, and implement the said project as early as possible.

Lastly, the Acting Municipal Treasurer be oriented with the preparation of the Cash Flow Chart for her to easily monitor available cash for certain appropriation, as well as safeguard bank books, receipts and other bank documents, whether old or new as evidence of the LGU’s transactions.

Non-completion by the Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count and reconcile the result of the count with the property and accounting records of the Property Custodian and Accounting Unit.

Management to:

We recommended that Management require the following to:

c. Monitor the delivery of goods to the awarded suppliers and impose liquidated damages at least equal to one-tenth (1/10) of one percent (1%) of the cost of the delayed goods scheduled for delivery for every day of delay until such goods are finally delivered and accepted by the procuring entity concerned; and

a. To check the records on the items listed in Appendix 8 if items already delivered and closely coordinate each other for proper reporting in the Financial Reports at Year End.
